subject: Product Management
title: BlackBerry's Lost Fit
description: A short lesson to help you apply BlackBerry's Lost Fit.

hook.text: In 2009, BlackBerry owned 50% of the US smartphone market. By 2016, its market share was effectively zero.

buildup.text: BlackBerry had perfect PMF for business email. Its physical keyboard and security features were unmatched.

discovery.text: Then the market shifted. The iPhone redefined what a smartphone was — from email tool to pocket computer.

twist.text: BlackBerry didn't lose customers to a better email device. It lost them to an entirely different product category.

climax.text: BlackBerry's story proves that PMF is tied to market conditions. When the market moves, your fit can vanish overnight.

punchline.text: You can have PMF today and lose it tomorrow. Markets move.

quiz.question: Why did BlackBerry lose product-market fit?
options: The market shifted from email devices to pocket computers — their fit became irrelevant
Their phones had too many technical problems
